single_runner_caveat: Single rater (Claude). Public API + commit history evidence only — no test execution, no internal-implementation reading, no multi-rater consensus. See triage-rubric.md for full caveats.

decisions.summary: _value(forBucketContents:) (UInt64) -> Int? × _bucketContents(for:) (Int?) -> UInt64 — codec by name + asymmetric (T) -> U / (U) -> T type-shape; matches cycle-6's _HashTable internal-pair acceptance posture (cf. cycle-6 pick #5)
exp(_:) × expMinusOne(_:) — both forward; expMinusOne(z) = exp(z) - 1; cycle-6 pick #7 (reject) rate-stability check
exp(_:) × cosh(_:) — cross-product; cosh(z) = (exp(z) + exp(-z))/2 is forward, not inverse of exp; cycle-6 pick #8 (reject)
exp(_:) × sinh(_:) — cross-product; both forward exponential-family functions
exp(_:) × log(_:) — genuine canonical inverse pair on principal branch (mod 2πi); cycle-6 pick #11 (accept) rate-stability confirmation
exp(_:) × sqrt(_:) — forward-forward cross-product; sqrt(exp(z)) = exp(z/2), not z

expMinusOne(_:) × log(_:) — structural near-miss; expMinusOne's true inverse is log(onePlus:) (cf. pick #15), not log; the numerical-variant pair was deliberately split into two functions to avoid loss-of-precision near zero, and the rubric requires the functions to be by-design inverses
cosh(_:) × sinh(_:) — both forward hyperbolic; cycle-6 pick #10 (reject)
cosh(_:) × acosh(_:) — genuine principal-branch inverse pair
sinh(_:) × asinh(_:) — genuine principal-branch inverse pair
tanh(_:) × atanh(_:) — genuine principal-branch inverse pair
cos(_:) × acos(_:) — genuine principal-branch inverse pair
sin(_:) × asin(_:) — genuine principal-branch inverse pair; cycle-6 pick #12 (accept) rate-stability confirmation
tan(_:) × atan(_:) — genuine principal-branch inverse pair
log(_:) × log(onePlus:) — two-overload forward pair; both compute logarithms of related arguments; not inverses of each other
log(_:) × sqrt(_:) — forward-forward cross-product
expMinusOne(_:) × sqrt(_:) — forward-forward cross-product involving accurate-variant exp
atan(_:) × atanh(_:) — same shape, related by analytic continuation (atanh(z) = -i·atan(iz)) but not functional inverses; both are inverse functions of their respective forward maps (tan, tanh)

endOfChunk(startingAt:) × startOfChunk(endingAt:) — genuine inverse pair on chunk-boundary domain (Base.Index restricted to chunk-start/end positions); cycle-6 pick #15 (accept) rate-stability confirmation. NB: this pick survives despite being slated for cycle-15 v1.18 suppression — the suppression target is usability (auto-emitted property tests need chunk-boundary generators) not correctness; rubric measures correctness
log(_:) × log(onePlus:) — two-overload forward pair on Double; same shape as CM pick #15
_description(type:) (String) -> String — formatter wraps input in structural format (e.g., 'OrderedSet<X>'); applying twice prepends the wrapper twice, so f(f(x)) ≠ f(x)

firstOccupiedBucketInChain(with:) (Bucket) -> Bucket — likely idempotent on already-occupied input but the function operates on _HashTable.UnsafeHandle (a stateful view); whether bucket-chain content is invariant between calls is internal-state dependent and read-only-via-source-code per rubric
exp(_:) — exp(exp(z)) = e^(e^z) ≠ exp(z); cycle-6 pick #21 (reject)
log(_:) — log(log(z)) ≠ log(z); cycle-6 pick #22 (reject)
sin(_:) — sin(sin(z)) ≠ sin(z) generally; non-trivial composition
asin(_:) — asin(asin(z)) ≠ asin(z); same logic as sin
tanh(_:) — tanh(tanh(z)) is contractive (approaches 0); ≠ tanh(z)
sqrt(_:) — sqrt(sqrt(z)) = z^(1/4) ≠ z^(1/2) generally; idempotent only at fixed points 0 and 1
endOfChunk(startingAt:) — boundary advances per call; endOfChunk(endOfChunk(x)) is the next chunk's end, ≠ endOfChunk(x); cycle-6 pick #25 (reject) rate-stability

sizeOfChunk(offset:) (Int) -> Int — the 'offset' arg is a chunk-position index, not a value-to-round-trip; sizeOfChunk(sizeOfChunk(0)) treats a size-value as a position-index, which is type-correct but semantically nonsensical
log(_:) (Double) -> Double — log(log(x)) ≠ log(x); same logic as CM pick #24
nearMissLines(_:) ([String]?) -> [String]? — function name suggests filtering/extraction but behavior on already-near-miss input is internal; cycle-6 pick #28 (unknown) rate-stability

index(_:offsetBy:) (Int, Int) -> Int — labels suggest position-vs-offset directionality; rubric mandates reject on directional argument-label patterns regardless of underlying integer-arithmetic commutativity; cycle-6 pick #29 (reject)
distance(from:to:) (Int, Int) -> Int — anti-commutative by definition: distance(b, a) = -distance(a, b); cycle-6 pick #30 rate-stability
index(_:offsetBy:) (Int, Int) -> Int — same shape + verdict as pick #33 (different namespace; OrderedSet vs OrderedDictionary)
-(z:w:) (Complex, Complex) -> Complex — subtraction is anti-commutative; cycle-6 pick #32 (reject)
_relaxedAdd(_:_:) (Self, Self) -> Self — abstract addition commutes; FP rounding caveat applies but is the standard approximate-equality concern; cycle-6 pick #33 (accept) rate-stability confirmation
index(_:offsetBy:) (Int, Int) -> Int — integer-offset addition associates; (i + n) + m = i + (n + m); cycle-6 pick #34 (accept) rate-stability confirmation

distance(from:to:) (Int, Int) -> Int — distance(distance(a, b), c) = c - (b-a) ≠ distance(a, distance(b, c)) = (c-b) - a; sign mismatch on a; not associative; cycle-6 pick #35 rate-stability (different reasoning, same conclusion)
index(_:offsetBy:) (Int, Int) -> Int — same shape + verdict as pick #38 (different namespace)
/(z:w:) (Complex, Complex) -> Complex — division is non-associative: (a/b)/c = a/(bc) ≠ a/(b/c) = ac/b
_relaxedMul(_:_:) (Self, Self) -> Self — multiplication associates; FP rounding caveat (same posture as pick #37 for addition)
minimumCapacity(forScale:) (Int) -> Int — capacity-from-scale is genuinely monotonic; cycle-6 pick #39 (accept) rate-stability confirmation

_description(type:) (String) -> String — string-prefix-wrapping preserves lex order monotonically only if the function is a pure wrap with no input transformation (case normalisation, escaping); without source-read the rater cannot verify the wrap-only assumption; rubric mandates unknown on genuine ambiguity
index(after:) (Int) -> Int — Collection-protocol increment, strictly monotonic on Int
sizeOfChunk(offset:) (Int) -> Int — chunk sizes vary by chunk position (not monotonic in offset); same site as idempotence pick #30

walkCap(for:) (C) -> Int — generic on C: BidirectionalCollection; the Comparable codomain Int is well-defined but the comparison on the C input has no canonical order (BidirectionalCollection has no Comparable conformance); whether the engine assumes count-based comparison is internal
format(_:) (CheckResult) -> String — string lex order ≠ enum semantic order; the format function destroys the input's semantic ordering; cycle-6 pick #42 (reject) rate-stability

endOfChunk(startingAt:) × startOfChunk(endingAt:) — genuine inverse pair on chunk-boundary domain; same verdict as round-trip pick #19; same correctness-vs-suppression note (cycle-15 stride-style label suppression target). Cycle-14 inverse-pair acceptance is 1/1 = 100%, but n=1 — interpret as 'the lone surviving pre-cycle-15 candidate is correctness-positive'

rescaledDivide(_:_:) × Complex.zero — division by zero is undefined (NaN/Inf in FP), not the input z; reverse direction rescaledDivide(0, z) = 0 ≠ z; identity-element property fails on both directions; cycle-6 pick #50 (reject) rate-stability confirmation
